commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=aa7780096a44067aed4c5ea28d18fa5f6e317adc)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Telegram outbound guard that blocks product/customer updates containing source paths or dense technical vocabulary and adds regression tests for blocked and allowed examples.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection, if the PR diff is applied: a Telegram message containing a product-context word plus three jargon terms reaches `assertTelegramProductPlainLanguage` and throws before `sendText` or `sendPayload`. I did not execute tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: This needs maintainer product-scope judgment and contributor real-behavior proof rather than an automated repair.

target.title: [codex] Guard technical Telegram product updates
target.body:
## Summary

- Add a central Telegram outbound guard for product/customer updates that contain source paths or dense implementation vocabulary.
- Block those messages before platform send, so they can be rewritten as user-visible outcomes, decisions, and risks.
- Add regression coverage for blocked technical updates and allowed customer-language updates.

## Why

OpenClaw already has message sending hooks and user-side presend guidance, but a central Telegram send path should not rely on every local wrapper or hook registration being present. Product/customer status updates are user-facing and should not degrade into source paths, API payloads, config detail, or implementation logs.

## Real behavior proof

This change was first applied as local containment on a live OpenClaw 2026.5.18 setup used for Telegram group/forum replies.

After the installed runtime patch and safe gateway restart:

- `openclaw gateway status --json` showed the gateway running as a new active process on OpenClaw 2026.5.18.
- The installed Telegram delivery bundle contained `assertTelegramProductPlainLanguage`, confirming the restarted gateway loaded the patched delivery code.
- The local presend regression suite passed with 19/19 checks, including blocked technical product-update examples and allowed user-facing product-language examples.
- A dry-run technical checkout-style update was blocked by the product-language guard, while a plain customer-language checkout update passed.

The local hotfix remains temporary containment only; this PR is the durable upstream path so future installs do not rely on a manual runtime patch.

## Validation

- `pnpm exec oxlint src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts --testNamePattern 'product updates|message_sending hooks|hooks cancel'`
- `git diff --check`
target.author: moeedahmed
